name: v2.2.15-beta (Build 2235)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.15-beta

> Build **2234-2235**

**Updates no longer make you re-download the whole app.** Until now, every new version meant fetching the full installer again the same ~200 MB, even when the actual change was a bug fix in a single file. From now on, the update popup offers **Update Now**, which downloads *only the files that actually changed* typically **a few megabytes instead of hundreds** verifies each one, and restarts into the new version by itself. The reason it can be this small: most of what ships with the app is the .NET runtime it runs on, and that part is identical between releases, so there is no reason to send it to you again. Your existing download links are untouched and still there for anyone who wants them, and they remain the fallback for anything the quick update can't do. Nothing is applied unless every downloaded file passes an integrity check, and if the update fails part-way, your installation is put back exactly as it was and the app restarts normally.

**One catch, and it only applies once:** this version cannot update *itself* the new way you'll install it the usual way, one last time. Every version after this one updates in seconds.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- The "Update Available" popup now leads with an **Update Now** button showing exactly how much it needs to download (e.g. *"Only 12.4 MB downloads just the changed files"*). Press it and the app fetches those files, checks each one against a published fingerprint, closes, swaps them in, and reopens on the new version. No browser, no installer, no clicking through setup.
- The **CDN Server** and **Download from Website** links are exactly where they were. If the quick update isn't available for a release, or anything goes wrong, they're what you'll see the app never leaves you without a way to update.
- Before anything is replaced, the app makes a copy of each original file. If any step fails a locked file, antivirus, a full disk everything is put back and the app restarts on the version you already had. If it's interrupted by a crash or power loss, it repairs itself on the next launch.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Settings → Appearance → Theme → Light**. Pure black-on-white across every window, card, modal, and page applied live, remembered across restarts, available in all 8 languages. Dark stays the default.
- The app compares its own files against a fingerprint list published with each release and downloads only the differences. Available for **installed** versions; the portable build continues to use the zip download.

name: v2.2.14-beta (Build 2233)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.14-beta

> Build **2220-2232**

## 🐛 Fixed

- the app's VPK extraction tool (`HLExtract.exe`) needs the Visual C++ 2010 runtime, which isn't installed on clean Windows 10/11 systems. Without it, you'd get *"Failed to extract pak01_dir.vpk using HLExtract"* with no hint that a missing DLL was the real cause. The required runtime DLLs are now bundled directly with the app no extra installs needed.
- right after the app found your Dota 2 folder, it checked online for a newer mod pack and waited for that check to finish before letting you click anything. On a bad network, or when the download servers were down, that meant minutes of a frozen-looking app with no spinner and no way to cancel. The check now runs in the background: the buttons work straight away, and the check itself gives up quickly instead of grinding through three servers. If a newer pack is found, you still get the same offer to update.
- the check only ran when you used **Auto Detect** or **Manual Detect** so if the app remembered your Dota 2 path from last time (nearly everyone with mods installed), it never once told you a newer pack was out. It does now.
- the app recorded "this version is installed" *before* it finished building the pack. If the app was killed or crashed in between, it would later see that record, compare it against the server, and report **up to date** on an install that never completed. It now only records the version once the pack is actually built and in place.
- if it couldn't open your browser, nothing happened at all no message, no error, just a button that appeared to do nothing. It now tells you when it fails.

- turning mods off restores two of Dota's own files, but the app changed the first one **before** downloading the clean copy it needs for the second. If it couldn't reach the download servers the most common way this fails you were left with one file reverted and one still modded: the broken combination the app flags as a risky patch state. It now downloads everything first, so a failure leaves your game files completely untouched.
- if the app couldn't write the restored game file (Dota 2 or antivirus holding it open), it reported success anyway while your mods stayed active. If you had chosen **Delete Permanently**, it went on to delete your mod files on the strength of that false success. It now falls back to a safe alternative, and only reports success when your mods really are off.
- the status light simply stayed on **Ready**, so a disable that didn't work looked exactly like one you never clicked. Failures now show a message including the case where mods are switched off but the files can't be deleted because Dota 2 is still running.
- **Disabling no longer leaves stray temporary files** in your Dota 2 folder if it fails partway (2230).

- if the patch couldn't reach the download server the most common way it fails it left a stray temporary file behind in the game folder. The patch now prepares and downloads everything *before* it writes anything, so a failure leaves your game files completely untouched.
- when the app detected a bad patch state, the sidebar button stayed its normal colour instead of turning red. It now shows it.

- in the confirmation popup, Enter always triggered **Continue** even when you had tabbed to **Cancel** and it was the highlighted button. Enter now does what the focused button says.
- switching language in Settings reset the version number at the bottom of the sidebar and the mod status label back to their startup placeholders ("Checking…", "Not Checked"), even though the status light stayed correct. Both now keep their real values.
- the little progress markers on the guide card weren't drawing at all. Fixed.
- you could close it with ✕ or by clicking outside, but not with Escape like every other popup. Escape now closes it, and Enter closes it too.

- in rare cases Dota 2 or antivirus holding the file a failed replace could wipe out your working package with nothing to restore. The app now backs up the current package before replacing it, so a locked file now just tells you to close Dota 2 and try again instead of losing your mods.
- dismissing it a certain way, or waiting too long, could leave the app hanging for up to a minute or eat your next click with nothing happening. Both are fixed.
- the button was only offered on success, so a failure gave you no way to see what happened. It now works on failures too.
- a first-time download now tells you it's in progress instead of sitting silently, and problems that used to fail silently (a locked file, a failed game-file update) are now reported instead of hidden behind a false "success."

---

## 🎨 Improved

- **The restart prompt after deleting your mods is in your language** (2230): it was a plain Windows box, always in English, with a single **OK** you couldn't decline. It's now an in-app card like every other dialog, translated in all 8 languages, and you can choose **Restart Now** or **Later**. The Disable Options dialog also shows the proper logo in its title bar.

- **"Update Detected" now speaks your language** (2229): the orange status shown when the app notices a Dota 2 update was hard-coded in English; it's now translated in all 8 supported languages.
- **The mod status checker got a spring clean** (2229): a large amount of status-checking code that never actually ran was removed, and the app now uses one shared status checker instead of three separate copies. Nothing changes in what you see but every status decision (Ready, Update Required, Disabled, Not Installed, and the broken-patch warning that guards against a VAC-risk state) is now covered by automated tests, so future updates can't silently break them.

- **Notifications wait while you read them** (2227): the small pop-up cards in the corner disappeared on a fixed timer even if you were mid-sentence. Hovering one now keeps it on screen; it dismisses shortly after you move away.
- **The banner no longer skips ahead while the window is hidden** (2227): minimise the app and come back, and the banner used to have moved on without you. It now pauses while the window isn't visible, and a banner image that fails to load is simply left out instead of showing a broken-image icon.

- **Skin Selector's "Show Log" now looks like the rest of the app** (2225): the button (added last build) opened in a separate popup window with its own look. It now opens inline, in the same style as the Miscellaneous log no functional change, just a consistent look everywhere you can view a log.

- **A failed install could delete your mod pack if Dota 2 was open** (2220): Before an install, the app moves your current mod pack aside so it can restore it if anything goes wrong. If Dota 2 was running and holding that file open, the "move aside" quietly failed and then, if the install itself failed, the cleanup deleted the file with no backup to put back, even though the app said your previous install was restored. Now the app only ever deletes or restores files it actually managed to back up first, so your working install is never destroyed. (Best practice is still to close Dota 2 before installing.)
- **"Checking version…" could hang for up to 5 minutes** (2220): The step that looks up the latest ModsPack had no time limit of its own, so a slow or stalled server could freeze the very start of an install. It now gives up after 60 seconds and cancelling is instant.

---

## ⚡ Faster

- The downloaded package is checksum-verified the moment it arrives; the app used to then re-check the same multi-hundred-megabyte file a second time for nothing. That redundant pass is gone, shaving several seconds off every install.
- Building your pack applies data one hero item at a time, and each step paused for a sliver that Windows rounds up to ~15 ms which added up to seconds on a full pack. That pause is now effectively free. (Also speeds up Skin Selector generation, which shares the same step.)

name: v2.2.13-beta (Build 2219)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.13-beta

> Build **2214-2219**

Install ModsPack now tells you exactly what happened: a proper completion card with a **Show Log** button when it succeeds, and a failure card that shows the actual reason when it doesn't in plain language, like "check your internet connection" or "check free disk space". Miscellaneous mods get the same treatment: the useless "Generation Failed / Extraction failed." message is gone, and a damaged mod package now **repairs itself** instead of dead-ending. This build also fixes a serious one: a failed or cancelled install could **delete** a Dota 2 core file instead of restoring it. Plus the Medium performance preset no longer blacks out the map on hover.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- Until now, the mod files behind every hero set sat on our CDN as ordinary zips anyone with a link could grab them without the app. They're now encrypted, and only ArdysaModsTools can unpack them. Nothing changes for you: sets download, verify and apply exactly as before.
- If your installed mod package gets damaged an interrupted write, a bad disk, antivirus chewing on it generating Miscellaneous mods with **Add to Current** used to just fail and tell you to go re-run it yourself in "Generate Only" mode. Now it detects the damage, **rebuilds the package from a clean base with your selections applied**, and finishes successfully. It tells you afterwards that your hero sets need applying again (the damaged package's contents can't be recovered but nothing is lost that wasn't already gone).
- Miscellaneous generation could only ever say "Extraction failed." no reason, no code, nothing to send us. It turned out the entire feature was writing its diagnostics to nowhere. Failures now name the step that broke, show an error code, and save the full details to `ardysa_fallback.log` for when you ask for help.
- I've found and fixed a bug where a file operation that failed **part-way** could leave one of Dota's core files **missing** rather than restored, because the very operation that failed was skipped during the undo. Installs now roll back completely and safely, whether they fail, are cancelled, or are interrupted mid-write. The manual install also patches both core files **together** now — previously a failure could leave one file updated and the other not, which quietly broke mod loading.
- Installing the ModsPack (Auto or Manual) now ends with a clear in-app card instead of a small toast. On success: a green **Complete** card with **Done** and **Show Log** press Show Log to see what was installed, including every customized hero and what was downloaded. On failure: a red card that opens with the reason already visible and a **Copy Log** button for sharing when you ask for help.
- Instead of "Installation failed. Check the console.", the card now says what actually went wrong and what to do "Download failed check your internet connection and try again", "The downloaded package was corrupted please try again", "Could not unpack the download check free disk space and antivirus", "verify game files in Steam". And when a failed install rolls back, the card tells you your previous install was restored.

---

## ✨ Added

- A damaged mod package is rebuilt from scratch automatically, with your Miscellaneous selections re-applied, instead of failing. You're told when it happens and reminded to re-apply your hero sets.
- Done + Show Log actions after every successful Auto/Manual install or reinstall; the log lists each step and hero in simple terms no technical noise.
- Failed installs open a card with the failure log and one-click **Copy Log**, in all 8 languages.

---

## 🐛 Fixed

- Whatever went wrong a corrupted package, a missing tool, a wedged unpacker you got "Generation Failed / Extraction failed." and nothing else. Every failure now names the step that broke, shows an error code, and writes the real details (including the unpacking tool's own output) to `ardysa_fallback.log`.
- The safety net that undoes a failed install skipped the exact step that failed so if a write broke half-way through, the file it was replacing could end up **gone**. Fixed, along with two related holes: undoing twice could itself delete the restored file, and cancelling between steps left earlier changes applied. Covered by a new regression test.
- The two Dota core files are now updated as one all-or-nothing step. Before, a failure after the first file was written left your game with one new file and one old one mods would silently fail to load.
- Closing the progress window now shows a neutral "Installation Canceled" message instead of a red failure card, and confirms your previous setup is untouched.
- Install and Disable now ignore a second click while the first is still running including while the install-method dialog is open, which is where the duplicate used to slip through.
- If the packaging tool wedges, the install now gives up after a bounded wait instead of freezing with no way out.
- The downloaded ModsPack is now verified against its published checksum *before* it's put into place, so a bad copy from a CDN is rejected rather than unpacked.
- The Medium preset combined a shadow setting that doesn't work with its shadow-texture size, so hovering over a hero, creep, or any NPC could black out the entire map. Medium now uses the same safe shadow setting as the Competitive preset same performance, no more black screen. **If you already applied Medium, re-apply it (or press DELETE) on the Performance tab to clear the old setting from your config.**

---

name: v2.2.12-beta (2213)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.12-beta

> Build **2209-2213**

Your Dota 2 folder is now a first-class setting: a new **Dota 2** card in Settings shows exactly where the app is pointed, with a one-click **Change** button and an **Auto-detect** switch. On a fresh install the app now finds Dota 2 for you on first launch, and a quick green "Dota 2 path found" confirmation shows you the folder it attached.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **See and change your Dota 2 folder in Settings** (2212): A new **Dota 2** section shows the current install path (with the important tail always visible) and a **Change** button that opens the folder picker right at your current path pick a new "dota 2 beta" folder and everything re-syncs instantly. Handy if you have multiple installs or moved the game.
- **Finds Dota 2 for you on first launch** (2212): When no path is saved yet, the app auto-detects your Dota 2 install at startup instead of waiting for you to press **Auto Detect** controlled by a new **Auto-detect Dota 2 Path** toggle (on by default). Your own saved or hand-picked path is never overridden.
- **Clear confirmation of the attached folder** (2212): A brief green "Dota 2 path found" banner shows the exact folder whenever the path is detected or changed, so you always know what the tools are pointed at.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Dota 2 install-path card** (2212): Current path, **Change** button (picker opens at the current folder), and an **Auto-detect Dota 2 Path** toggle all in a new Dota 2 section at the top of Settings. Available in all 8 languages.
- **Startup auto-detect** (2212): First launch attaches your Dota 2 install automatically when no path is saved.

---

## 🛠️ Changed

- **Auto Detect grays out once a folder is attached** (2212): After the app has a valid Dota 2 path, **Auto Detect** is done and turns non-clickable while **Manual Detect** stays available so you can always re-point to a different install.
- **A clearer message when Dota 2 isn't found** (2212): If startup auto-detect comes up empty, a warning now points you at the highlighted detect buttons instead of failing silently.

---

name: v2.2.11-beta (Build 2208)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.11-beta

> Build **2204-2208**

Manual install now verifies where a pack comes from before installing it: official packs install as before, while packs from other creators previously rejected are accepted with a notice. Verification is instant and fully offline. Plus install now recovers on its own from a corrupted download, and WebView2 profile self-healing from build 2204.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- Official status is decided from the pack itself with no network call and no waiting the check runs off a single fast content listing. A shared pack you generated yourself no longer gets flagged with a scary warning; it's simply treated as your own pack and installs on confirmation.
- When you pick a `pak01_dir.vpk`, the app checks its origin. Official packs install exactly as before, and packs from other creators previously rejected are now **accepted** with an **"Unofficial pack"** notice that makes clear bugs from such packs are not ArdysaMods' responsibility. Nothing installs without your confirmation.
- Packs without the ArdysaMods signature are installed as-is the app no longer rejects them, and it doesn't overwrite their data with ArdysaMods package data either.
- If the browser profile gets corrupted or locked (e.g. after a crash or force-close), the app now resets it automatically on the next launch instead of throwing an error.

---

## ✨ Added

- The origin check no longer contacts the server at all it reads the pack's own contents in a single fast pass, so it's instant whether you're online or off. A pack you generated yourself is recognised as your own and installs without an alarming "modified" warning.
- The verdict decides the flow: official → rebuild pipeline; unofficial → warning, installed as-is. Available in all 8 languages.
- All source files standardized to LF line endings across the repository.

---

## 🐛 Fixed

- If `Core Package` downloaded incompletely, the install used to fail on every retry and needed a manual cache wipe to unstick. It now clears the bad download automatically and re-fetches a clean copy on the next run.
- The public `Signature` file is now only a decoy copying it into another pack grants nothing. Official status is decided by a separate, non-obvious marker that isn't advertised, so a hand-assembled pack can't fake it.
- The origin check requires an exact match at an exact location packs with lookalike file names no longer slip through.
- `CoreWebView2Environment.CreateAsync()` throws when the EBWebView profile is corrupted or stale-locked. The helper now catches the failure, renames the bad profile folder, and retries so a one-time reset fixes what used to require manual folder deletion.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.10-beta (Build 2203)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.10-beta

> Build **2200-2203**

Install robustness and consistency pass: failed or cancelled installs now **restore your previous working mod pack** instead of deleting it, and every install fat or slim goes through the same rebuild pipeline so the output is deterministic.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- Setup no longer asks you to install the WebView2 runtime when it's already on your PC it now checks for it exactly the way the app does, so the two can't disagree.
- If a mod install or rebuild fails (or you cancel it), the previous working `pak01_dir.vpk` is restored instead of being deleted. You keep your mods while retrying.
- On smaller screens, choosing a bigger **UI Size** could crop pages, dialogs, and buttons. The whole interface now scales to fit the screen, and anything still too tall scrolls instead of clipping.
- Dropping a `.vpk` onto the manual-install zone was showing a "blocked" cursor. It now accepts the file (and keeps working even when the app is running as administrator).
- The "Building game data…" step used to sit on one line for a long time and look stuck. It now advances a real progress bar with an `Applying package data… N%` readout as it works through the package and language files.
- : Both legacy "fat" packs and modern slim packs now go through the same strip → rebuild pipeline, guaranteeing a deterministic, up-to-date package regardless of what was downloaded.
- Choosing a custom location now always installs into an `ArdysaModsTools` subfolder, and the uninstaller refuses to recursively delete drive roots or common folders like Desktop and Documents — even if something points it there.
- The old version is only removed after the new files are unpacked and verified, so a corrupt or interrupted download leaves your existing install untouched.
- Removing the app from Windows "Add or remove programs" no longer briefly flashes the installer window, and the uninstaller now reliably cleans itself up afterward.

---

## ✨ Added

- Like Skin Selector and Miscellaneous, the Install action can now be temporarily turned off from the server. If it's off you'll see a notice with a **Join Discord** button for updates.
- A new **Utilities** entry appears in the sidebar under a new **Extra** group, shown greyed-out with a `SOON` tag until the page is ready.

## 🐛 Fixed

- The installer used to check for the WebView2 runtime differently than the app, so it could offer to install one that was already there. It now uses the same check as the app, ending the false prompt. (No change to what gets downloaded — it still uses Microsoft's small WebView2 setup only when the runtime is genuinely missing.)
- Canceling or a failed rebuild now restores the previously working `pak01_dir.vpk` and hash marker instead of deleting them, so you don't lose your mods on a retry.
- Legacy "fat" mod packs that bundled their own `package` and localization are now stripped and repacked with the current game data, fixing cases where an old pack's stale `package` survived into the installed VPK.
- Pages, dialogs, and cards no longer get cut off when UI Size is set to Large or Extra Large — the app scales to fit and scrolls when needed.
- Dropping a `.vpk` file onto the manual-install zone now works (previously showed a blocked cursor), including when the app runs elevated.
- The game-data build step now reports a live percentage across its phases instead of a single static line.
- Custom paths are kept inside an app subfolder, and every delete step is guarded against drive roots and well-known user folders.
- Cleanup of the old version now happens only after the new one is verified.
- Fixed the quiet uninstall UI flash, a crash when launching a second installer, and the leftover uninstaller after removal.

## 🛠️ Changed

- Every install now follows the same strip → rebuild pipeline regardless of pack type. The `VpkBundlesItemsGameAsync` shortcut is removed.
- The banner now reads "Dota 2 is still running — tools are unavailable until you close the game."
- The game-data build no longer logs internal file paths or block counts.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.9-beta (Build 2199)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.9-beta

> Build **2195-2199**

Fixes for the **Skin Selector** and **Miscellaneous** pages so generating always works — including a crash that could freeze and close the app during generation.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Fixed a generation crash (freeze & force-close)**: Generating could freeze and close the app — most often with a Skin Selector preset that had a **Base** selected but no set on a hero, which pops the "no set selected" confirmation. That confirmation could crash the app right after you clicked it. Generation now runs through cleanly, whatever you have selected.
- **New sets work as soon as they're uploaded**: Picking a brand-new set and generating no longer fails while the set is still syncing — the app uses the set's own bundled index if the cloud copy isn't ready yet.
- **The Generate button is never stuck**: On slow or blocked connections the "preparing thumbnails" screen could stay up and block **Generate ModsPack**. It now clears after a few seconds and finishes loading thumbnails in the background, so you can always start generating.
- **Connection heads-up on startup**: If Downloads or Generate get stuck, the console now points out that it's usually a regional CDN connection issue and suggests trying again, switching networks, or using a free VPN (Cloudflare WARP) — so you know it isn't the app.
- **Leaner, faster-to-maintain codebase** (from 2195): Removed the old fallback interface and a large amount of dead code — with no impact on the features you use. A follow-up pass (2198) trimmed a further ~2,770 lines of unused code and retired the now-unreachable legacy dialogs.
- **WebView2 is now required** (from 2195): The modern interface is the only interface. If the Microsoft Edge WebView2 Runtime is missing, the app shows a clear message with an install link. The installer already bundles this runtime, so most users are unaffected.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Startup connectivity notice**: A highlighted line in the console at launch explains that stuck downloads or a stuck **Generate** are usually a regional CDN connection issue, and suggests retrying, switching networks, or using the free Cloudflare WARP VPN app. Shown in all 8 supported languages.

## 🐛 Fixed

- **Generation could freeze and close the app**: A crash during generation — triggered by an on-screen confirmation dialog (most reliably the "Base selected but no set" prompt in the Skin Selector) — could freeze the app and force it to close. Fixed; generation now completes normally regardless of which sets or bases are selected.
- **Skin Selector — new sets couldn't generate**: A set uploaded moments earlier could fail to generate for some users until the server finished syncing. It now falls back to the set's bundled data and works immediately.
- **Skin Selector & Miscellaneous — couldn't click Generate**: On certain connections the thumbnail-loading screen never went away, making the Generate button unclickable. It now times out gracefully and loads thumbnails in the background instead.

## 🗑️ Removed (build 2195)

- **Legacy interface**: The old WinForms fallback UI and its unused components were removed.
- **Dead code & an unused dependency**: Dropped orphaned dialogs, services, methods, and the unused image-processing package.

## 🛠️ Changed (build 2195)

- **Startup**: Now verifies the WebView2 Runtime up front and shows a localized message (in all 8 supported languages) if it's missing.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.8-beta (Build 2194)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.8-beta

> Builds **2189-2194**

This release modernizes the confirm dialogs into in-shell modals, sharpens Skin Selector and Miscellaneous generation, and adds quality-of-life touches.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **In-shell confirm modals**: Confirm Generation and Confirm Install now use shell-style WebView modals instead of native boxes.
- **Smarter Skin Selector**: Skipped/failed sets are surfaced in the completion dialog and live log, with a saved per-run report.
- **More Miscellaneous categories**: Cursor, ancient, roshan, announcer, and mega_kills are now available.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Support — daily snooze**: "Don't show this today" hides the on-launch support prompt for the day.
- **Miscellaneous — Clear button**: Reset all options to default in one click.
- **Skin Selector — generation report**: Per-run report kept for the last 10 runs.

## 🛠️ Changed

- **Confirm dialogs**: Confirm Generation and Confirm Install rebuilt as in-shell WebView modals (native fallback on the classic form).
- **Logging**: User-facing logs now say "package" instead of `items_game.txt`.

## 🐛 Fixed

- **Skin Selector**: Fixed thumbnails and a generate `STATUS_BREAKPOINT` crash.
- **Miscellaneous**: Short block IDs now resolve the correct item instead of colliding.
- **Manual VPK drag-drop**: Reliable single-`.vpk` drop over the dropzone.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.7-beta (Build 2188)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.7

> Builds **2184-2188**

This release focuses on localization, drag-and-drop improvements, high DPI scaling alignment, and UI refinements. The Dota 2 Performance Tweak page is now fully localized across all 8 supported languages with live dynamic culture updates, you can now drag-and-drop `.vpk` and `.zip` mod files directly into the client, and the main shell has been widened to give navigation elements more breathing room.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Full Localization for Dota 2 Performance** — The tweak page categories, options, dropdown values, and descriptions are now fully translated across all 8 supported languages with instant live-switching.
- **Drag & Drop Mod Import** — Import mods directly by dragging and dropping `.vpk` or `.zip` files onto the Install Method WebView dialog.
- **Widened Main Shell** — The main window size has been expanded to `1280x780` with a wider sidebar panel (`280px`) to prevent navigation labels from clipping or wrapping.
- **Better DPI scaling alignment** — Fixed layout clipping issues at high DPI settings by aligning WinForms host DPI scaling with WebView2 layout rendering.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Drag & Drop support** — Enabled importing `.vpk` or `.zip` files directly by dragging them onto the Install Method dialog.
- **Zip-nested VPK import** — Added automatic extraction and support for `pak01_dir.vpk` files nested inside `.zip` archives.
- **Dynamic UI Zoom** — Configurable user-controlled UI scaling (Normal/Large/Extra Large) applied on form load.
- **Dota 2 Performance live updates** — Bound performance tweak forms to active language changes so text updates dynamically without a restart.

## 🛠️ Changed

- **Wider sidebar & main shell** — Widened the main host window to `1280x780` and the sidebar to `280px` to fit non-English labels comfortably.
- **Brand logo in Performance Tweak** — Swapped out the lightning bolt icon in the performance tweak page title bar for the official Ardysa brand logo.
- **Localized host alerts** — Moved Patcher/Patcher toast alerts and status banners to the central localization system.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.6-beta (Build 2183)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.6-beta

> Builds **2181-2183**

This release trims the app down and speeds it up: the Miscellaneous and Skin Selector pages are now WebView2-only (a large chunk of old, unused code is gone), Miscellaneous generation no longer idles, and Dota 2 Performance gains a one-click way to remove your `autoexec.cfg`. The "What's New" promo card and ModsPack version can now also be updated from the server without shipping a new app build.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Snappier Miscellaneous generation** — Removed the artificial pacing delays, so generating mods finishes noticeably faster with no change to what's produced.
- **Remove your autoexec.cfg in one click** — Dota 2 Performance now has a control to delete the installed `autoexec.cfg`, with a clear confirmation and the page reset back to the recommended-but-not-written state.
- **Leaner app** — The legacy classic-WinForms versions of the Skin Selector and Miscellaneous pages (long superseded by the WebView2 UI) were removed, along with a large amount of dead code (~6,000+ lines).
- **Fresher promo card, no update needed** — The "Install ModsPack" card image and ModsPack version badge are now driven by a server config file, so they can be refreshed without releasing a new app version.

---

## 🛠️ Changed

- **Faster Miscellaneous generation** — About 1.5 s of built-in waiting was removed from the generation steps. Progress reporting is unchanged; it just no longer sits idle between steps.
- **WebView2-only Skin Selector & Miscellaneous** — These pages now run only on the modern WebView2 UI. If the WebView2 runtime isn't available, the page shows a short "requires the WebView2 runtime" notice instead of falling back to the old interface.
- **Up-to-date promo card** — The "Install ModsPack" card art is now refreshed from the CDN reliably, so a changed image shows up on the next launch instead of a stale cached copy.

## ✨ Added

- **Remove autoexec.cfg** — In Dota 2 Performance, you can now delete the `autoexec.cfg` that the app installed. You'll get a toast confirming it was removed (or that there was nothing to remove), and the page returns to showing the recommended preset values.

## 🗑️ Removed

- **Old classic UI** — The classic-WinForms Skin Selector and Miscellaneous windows and their unused supporting code were removed. The WebView2 versions have been the default for some time; this just clears out the parallel, unused implementation.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.5-beta (Build 2180)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.5-beta

> Build **2177-2180**

This release makes ArdysaModsTools **multilingual** — the entire interface can now run in 8 languages, switchable live from Settings — tightens up icon consistency so every window and the system tray show the Ardysa brand icon, and polishes the Skin Selector with style-group covers and a fix for assets bleeding between runs.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Speak your language** — The app now ships in **English, Spanish, German, French, Portuguese, Russian, Simplified Chinese, and Traditional Chinese**, switchable on the fly from **Settings → Appearance** with no restart.
- **Consistent branding everywhere** — The Skin Selector, Miscellaneous, and system-tray icons now all show the Ardysa app icon instead of the default Windows icon.
- **Cleaner update prompt** — The "Update Required" dialog now matches the app's monochrome shell instead of a plain Windows message box.
- **Tidier Skin Selector** — Multi-style sets can now show a single cover image, heroes without any sets are clearly greyed-out, and a fix stops leftover files from one generation bleeding into the next.

---

## ✨ Added

- **Multi-language support (8 languages)**: A single translation catalog now powers both the native windows and the WebView pages. English is the default and the fallback for anything not yet translated, so the app always stays readable. Dates, numbers, and currency follow the chosen language's regional format.
- **Language selector**: Pick your language under **Settings → Appearance**. The change applies immediately across the shell, Settings, and the localized dialogs.
- **Skin Selector — style covers**: Sets with multiple styles can now show a single cover image on the style card and preview, so a group reads as one cosmetic instead of a list of variants.
- **Skin Selector — heroes without sets are locked**: Heroes that don't have any sets yet are greyed-out and can't be selected, and the **Has Sets** filter is easier to reach.

## 🛠️ Changed

- **Unified app icon** — The Skin Selector and Miscellaneous windows now display the Ardysa icon in the taskbar and Alt-Tab, and the system-tray icon matches it too. Previously these fell back to the generic Windows icon.
- **In-shell "Update Required" prompt** — When Dota 2 has updated and the Patcher needs to run, the confirmation is now shown as an in-window modal styled to match the rest of the app, replacing the old native Yes/No message box.

## 🐛 Fixed

- **No more leftover files between generations** — The Skin Selector now builds each mod from a fresh copy of the base game files, so assets from one generation can no longer bleed into the next. (Your base files re-extract once, automatically, the first time you generate after updating.)
- **Strength / Universal icons** — The Strength and Universal attribute icons now render correctly in the hero gallery.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.4-beta (Build 2176)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.4-beta

> Covers builds **2170–2176**

This release is a visual overhaul: the **Skin Selector** and **Miscellaneous** pages are rebuilt on a single monochrome, terminal-style design system that matches the rest of the app and the website — plus a fix for a crash when dragging or closing those windows.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- The Skin Selector and Miscellaneous pages (and all of their dialogs) now share one clean black-and-white, JetBrains Mono design language with sharp corners, corner-bracket accents, and an animated grid backdrop.
- A wider window, larger square option cards, and a bigger option preview modal make browsing easier.
- Dragging or closing these windows no longer risks terminating the app.

---

## ✨ Added

- A new **Prismatic** category (sets whose archive filename starts with `prismatic_`) is now available per hero. Prismatic is a pure asset overlay — it ships no `index.txt` and contributes no `items_game.txt` blocks; it only merges its texture/model files. It is applied **first as the foundation** (strictly highest layer weight), so every other selection — Set, Items, and Base — overrides its files wherever they overlap. Prismatic coexists with your Set/Items/Base picks and only one can be active at a time. The selection is persisted with your last configuration (`HeroSelectionState.PrismaticIndex`).
- Prismatic is a Base add-on. It can only be enabled once a Base Hero is selected — the category is dimmed/locked with a "Select a Base Hero first" notice until then, clearing the Base automatically drops the Prismatic, and a stale selection carrying a Prismatic without a Base is dropped during plan building so the rule holds end-to-end (UI → presenter → generation).


## 🛠️ Changed

### Skin Selector

- Rebuilt the hero gallery and every modal (set picker, Style Preview, alert, confirm, loading overlay) in the monochrome terminal style. Set categories are now told apart by labels and bracketed tags instead of colors, and the active pick shows a white ring with corner brackets.
- Added a scrolling notice in the title bar: "Not all heroes have a set — sets will be updated gradually".

### Miscellaneous

- Rebuilt the page and all overlays (option carousel, styles, ethereal effects, progress console, and the mode/alert/tribute dialogs) in the same style.
- Wider window, larger and more-square option cards (the small selected-state square was removed — selection now reads from the white ring), and an enlarged option preview modal.

## 🐛 Fixed

- Dragging or closing the Skin Selector or Miscellaneous window could crash the app (`0xc000041d`). The borderless-drag and close calls are now deferred so they no longer run inside the WebView2 message callback.
- The Misc generation progress bar previously stalled at 60% because the build/install/finalize phase messages matched none of the progress keywords. The phase-to-percentage mapping now covers the full pipeline (Validating → conflict check → Preparing/Extracting → Applying/Fetching → Building/Recompiling → Installing → Finalizing → Complete) and uses `Math.Max`, so the bar advances monotonically through every phase and never rewinds on out-of-order or repeated messages.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.3-beta (Build 2169)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.3-beta

> Covers builds **2161 → 2169**

This release refreshes the in-app experience: native Windows pop-ups are replaced with notifications styled to match the app shell, the Ardysa logo replaces the old glyph across the window and About page, and the Skin Selector now remembers your last selection.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Native dialogs → in-shell notifications** — The Skin Selector beta notice and the permanent "delete mod files" confirmation now appear as an in-window modal, and patch results show as toast notifications — all styled to match the app instead of as separate Windows dialogs and tray balloons.
- **Skin Selector remembers your last selection** — Your previous picks (each hero's set, items, and base override) are restored automatically the next time you open the Skin Selector.
- **Refreshed branding** — The Ardysa logo now appears in the title bar, the About page, and the What's New card.

---

## ✨ Added

### Skin Selector

- Your last generated configuration (per-hero set, items, and base override) is saved when you generate and automatically restored when you reopen the Skin Selector, so your previous selection is highlighted and visible again. Previously only which heroes were highlighted was remembered, not their actual picks.

### Notifications

- A short countdown now runs before you can dismiss the startup Support dialog or confirm the Skin Selector beta notice, so the notice isn't skipped instantly.

## 🛠️ Changed

- **In-app notifications** — Native message boxes and tray balloons were replaced with shell-styled components: an in-window confirmation modal (Skin Selector beta notice and the permanent "delete mod files" confirmation) and toast notifications for patch results (**Patch Complete** / **Already Up To Date** / **Patch Failed**).
- **Branding** — The main window title bar and the About page (header and identity mark) now use the Ardysa logo in place of the previous glyph.
- **What's New card** — Now features the Ardysa logo, with the logo, title, and subtitle centered within the card.
- **Install ModsPack card** — The download icon is now a solid black tile with a white glyph for stronger contrast against the banner.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.2-beta (Build 2159)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.2-beta

> Covers builds **2157 → 2158**

This release focuses on decoupling the app from GitHub by reading the "What's New" feed from our public CDN, alongside important documentation updates for privacy and code signing compliance.

---

## 🌟 Highlights

- **Auth-Free Changelog Feed** — The in-app "What's New" feed now reads from a public, auth-free Cloudflare R2 mirror instead of relying entirely on the GitHub Releases API. This ensures the app's changelog continues working independently of GitHub repository visibility.
- **Privacy & Code Signing** — Added a dedicated Privacy Policy clarifying our zero-telemetry stance, and updated documentation to acknowledge the SignPath Foundation for providing our code signing infrastructure.

---

## 📰 What's New & Feed Updates

### 🛠️ Changed

- **Auth-Free R2 Feed** — The in-app changelog now attempts to fetch from a public R2 feed (`cdn.ardysamods.my.id/config/whatsnew.json`) with a short timeout, seamlessly falling back to the GitHub API only if necessary. This removes the hard dependency on the source repo being publicly readable.

---

## 📄 Documentation

### ✨ Added

- **Privacy Policy** — Added `PRIVACY.md` to formally document that ArdysaModsTools does not collect any user data, analytics, or telemetry.
- **Copyright** — Restructured `README.md` and added explicit copyright under Ardysa.

### 🛠️ Changed

- **SignPath Acknowledgments** — Added required references and acknowledgments to the SignPath Foundation in `README.md` to comply with open-source code signing requirements.

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.1-beta (Build 2156)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.1-beta
 
> Covers builds **2150 → 2156**
 
This release introduces multi-style support for cosmetic sets and items (collapsing variants into Style Cards), adds a dedicated Style Preview Modal, simplifies the gallery visual layout with category badges, and hardens the CDN fallback chain and hero database update pipeline.
 
---
 
## 🌟 Highlights
 
- **Style Cards & Preview Modal** — Sets and items can now ship multiple style variants, collapsing into a single card in the hero gallery. Clicking it opens a dedicated Style Preview Modal to choose between variant thumbnails and custom labels.
- **Self-Fixing Hero Database** — A **Check Database** / **Update Database** action pair in Settings verifies your local `heroes.json` against the live Cloudflare R2 origin via SHA-256, backed by a persistent local cache folder.
- **Simplified Card UI** — Replaced generic "Set N" labels on Legacy, Custom, Persona, and Base hero tiles with sleek, color-coded category badges (**Set**, **Mix**, **Persona**, and **Arcana**).
- **Hardened CDN Falling** — Pinned Cloudflare R2 as the primary origin and stripped cache-busting queries that caused HTTP 404s on fallback mirrors.
 
---
 
## 🎨 Skin Selector & Style Cards
 
### ✨ Added
 
- **Style Cards integration** — Sets/items can now bundle multiple styles (alternate visual variants with distinct archives and thumbnails) in `heroes.json`. Styled variants collapse into a single Style Card with style-chip selection in the modal.
- **Style Preview Modal** — Clicking on a collapsed Style Card tile now opens a modal overlay showing a grid of all available styles. Select or deselect individual variants, preview their thumbnails, and close the modal gracefully via click-outside or the `Escape` key.
- **Gallery Category Badges** — Legacy, Custom, Persona, and Base hero cards now render a compact, colored category tag badge (**Set**, **Mix**, **Persona**, **Arcana**) instead of generic text.
 
### 🛠️ Changed
 
- **Style-aware Python scanning** — `2-patch_models.py` scanner and model-patching functions are fully style-aware, scanning style groups under subfolders, handling CDNs, back-filling missing images, and pruning empty folders.
 
### 🐛 Fixed
 
- **Missing updates carousel items** — Fixed a bug where styled sets went missing in "Latest Updates" by recording the flattened representative key (`Set N (first style label)`) instead of the bare group key. A migration script repoints existing entries in `set_update.json`.
 
---
 
## 💾 Hero Database & Offline Caching
 
### ✨ Added
 
- **Database Self-Update** — A manual trigger pair in Settings to verify and update the local database against live copies via SHA-256, allowing users on blocked/impaired networks to fix missing thumbnails.
- **Persistent Local Cache** — Successfully-downloaded `heroes.json` databases are stored in `%LocalAppData%\ArdysaModsTools\data` alongside SHA-256/ETag cache files (`ManifestCache`), persisting across updates.
 
### 🛠️ Changed
 
- **Safe loading fallback** — Database load order now prioritizes CDN → persisted local cache → bundled fallback, ensuring reliability.
- **Bounded timeouts** — Thumbnail intercepts are bounded to 45s to prevent blocked CDN requests from hanging the WebView2 interface.
 
### 🐛 Fixed
 
- **Carousel image duplicates** — The "Latest Updates" carousel now hides updates that cannot resolve to a distinct thumbnail in the database, showing a neutral placeholder on error instead of duplicate portraits.
- **Partial cache logging** — The launch console now reports partial asset cache results (e.g. `412/435 (23 unavailable)`) on network failures instead of claiming "ready".
 
---
 
## 🌐 CDN Routing Hardening
 
### 🛠️ Changed
 
- **Authoritative R2 Pinning** — Cloudflare R2 is pinned at index 0 in the Smart CDN chain to bypass latency tests that prioritized mirrors with stale content.
 
### 🐛 Fixed
 
- **Fallback HTTP 404s** — Stripped cache-busting query strings (`?v=YYYYMMDDHH`) from CDN URLs, fixing connection failures on mirrors like GitHub Raw which don't support parameters.
 
---
 
## 🧹 Tooling & Cleanup
 
### 🗑️ Removed
 
- **Legacy CLI script mode** — Dropped the unused `item_namer` import, `vpk_path` configs, `sets_renamed` trackers, and duplicate CLI dispatch logic from `2-patch_models.py`.
 
---
 
_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: v2.2.0-beta (Build 2149)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.2.0-beta

> Covers builds **2136 → 2149**

This release rebuilds the main window as a WebView2 shell with a clean monochrome design, overhauls hero-skin generation into a predictable layered pipeline, makes thumbnails load instantly and offline, and hardens every download with retries and content verification.

---

## 🖥️ Main Window & UI

### ✨ Added

- **WebView2 main shell** — the main window is now a WebView2 hybrid (`MainFormWebView`), matching the rest of the app. The classic WinForms window is kept as an automatic fallback when the Edge WebView2 runtime is unavailable.
- **About dialog** — a new title-bar button opens a minimalist About page with the app identity, a short description, and Credits & Acknowledgments (author, community, third-party libraries, license).
- **What's New chooser** — opens a modal to pick **Changelog** (in-app GitHub releases) or **ModsPack** (a searchable, filterable grid of hero-skin updates from the site).
- **Version badges** — the What's New card shows the live app version and ModsPack version (served from `config/banner.json`).
- **Banner carousel** on the main shell, sourced from the R2 manifest.
- **Native dialogs** — Install Method and Disable Options were rebuilt as WebView2 dialogs.

### 🛠️ Changed

- **Monochrome redesign** of the main shell and Performance Tweaks page — black/white, JetBrains Mono, sharp corners.
- **Layout** — Disable Mods and Performance Tweak moved into the sidebar; Install ModsPack is now an image card; the console is cleaner with category-colored logs (success/error/warning/progress).
- **Performance Tweak** is now gated behind path detection — the button stays disabled until a Dota 2 install is known, since `autoexec.cfg` can't be resolved without it.
- **Onboarding guide** now works on the WebView2 shell as a DOM-native overlay.

### 🐛 Fixed

- **Settings / Tweaks / Support no longer crash or render blank** from the main shell. All WebView2 controls now share a single `CoreWebView2Environment`, and dialogs open on a clean message-loop turn instead of inside the WebView2 event handler (which previously hard-crashed the process).
- **Patch Update button** no longer spams `[STATUS] Ready: …` on every click — status logging is now driven by an actual status change.
- **Card fonts** — promo cards and a few stray controls now render in JetBrains Mono.

---

## 🎨 Skin Selector & Mod Generation

### ✨ Added

- **Persona support** — full hero-model replacement mods (`persona_` prefix) get a dedicated 5th section in the Skin Selection modal, with mutual exclusion that clears and disables Item/Base selections when a Persona is picked.
- **Per-hero base-priority** — an optional `method` field in `heroes.json` (`1` = Base first, `2` = Base last) explicitly overrides the `item_slot hero_base` auto-detection during generation.
- **Live `items_game.txt`** — every generation now extracts `items_game.txt` from your detected Dota 2 install instead of the bundled `Original.zip`, so mods stay aligned with the current game patch automatically with a clean, never-stale base.
- **Slot-tag badges** — item tiles show their slot tags (`[shoulder]`, `[weapon]`, …), with tag-based mutual exclusion so picking an item deselects others in the same slot.
- **Compatibility alert** — a confirmation prompt warns when a Base Hero mod is selected without a matching set.

### 🛠️ Changed

- **Layered last-writer-wins pipeline** — selections apply foundation→top (Base → Sets/Custom/Persona → Items). Every layer's `index.txt` blocks are written into `items_game.txt`, and a later, lower layer overrides earlier ones for the same item id — so the most specific pick wins its slot while non-overlapping slots from every layer still apply.
- **Structure-preserving patcher** — the authored `index.txt` block is applied **verbatim** (exact key order, nesting, and numbered modifiers preserved); only structurally essential vanilla keys the index omits are carried over.
- **MVP refactor** — generation moved out of `HeroGalleryForm` into a testable `HeroGalleryPresenter`; the form is now a thin WebView2 host.

### 🐛 Fixed

- **Dropped layers** — selected Sets/Items are no longer dropped when a higher-priority Base claims the same item ids (and a related regression that copied 0 asset files).
- **Inverted merge order** — fixed inverted layer sort weights causing incorrect merge priority.
- **Cancel reported as failure** — user-cancelled generation no longer pops a "Generation Failed" dialog; cancellation is now tracked with an explicit flag instead of a magic string.
- **Conflict resolution** — the WebView UI now shows the conflict-resolution dialog on critical conflicts, and resolved conflicts actually drop the losing mod from the generated VPK (previously a no-op).
- **Stale & hanging generation** — fixed the Skin Selector reading a stale side-channel selection, a "Base without set" confirmation that could hang generation forever (now bounded by a 60s timeout), and out-of-range selection indices.

---

## ⚡ Performance Tweaks & autoexec

### 🛠️ Changed

- **MVP refactor** — `Dota2PerformanceForm` was decomposed into `AutoexecService` and `Dota2PerformancePresenter`, with `autoexec.cfg` saved through `FileTransactionService` for atomic writes and rollback on failure.
- **Launch options persistence** — your launch-options selection is now remembered across sessions, with explicit copy-only labelling (the app cannot edit Steam's launch options).

### 🐛 Fixed

- **"Dota 2 cfg folder not found"** — Apply settings now resolves `game\dota\cfg` correctly and creates it on a fresh install. An explicit-but-invalid path now reports not-found instead of silently writing into an unrelated Dota 2 install.
- **Double rollback** — `ApplySettingsAsync` no longer rolls the file transaction back twice on failure.
- **Toast reliability** — load/apply/export toasts are now awaited (not fire-and-forget), and WebView2 bridge payloads are safely JSON-encoded.

---

## 🖼️ Thumbnails & Asset Caching

### ✨ Added

- **Launching State preloader** — right after the window opens, a throttled background task caches all gallery thumbnails into the persistent local cache, so Miscellaneous and Skin Selector open instantly and work offline. Reports concise progress and cancels cleanly on close.
- **Preload toggle** in Settings (default ON).

### 🛠️ Changed

- **Unified cache** — a `WebViewAssetInterceptor` now serves CDN image requests from the persistent asset cache (`%LocalAppData%\ArdysaModsTools\AssetCache`), so thumbnails download once and survive restarts, temp cleanup, and updates. The WebView2 user-data folder also moved out of `%TEMP%` to a persistent location.
- **Known-missing tracking** — assets with no CDN thumbnail (Default/Disable choices and a few not-yet-uploaded cosmetics) are remembered with a 7-day TTL, so the "Downloading thumbnails…" overlay only appears for genuinely new assets.

### 🐛 Fixed

- **Re-downloading on every open** — fixed thumbnails re-downloading after the app was closed, and the overlay re-appearing because of a sanitization mismatch between the C# preloader and the CDN/JS filename convention.
- **404 request storm** — opening a panel with missing thumbnails no longer floods the log with `HTTP 404`s or trips the CDN circuit breaker; known-missing assets return an instant placeholder.

---

## 🌐 Downloads, CDN & Security

### ✨ Added

- **SHA-256 content verification** (ADR-0010) — hero set zips, `Original.zip`, and the app installer/portable are verified against a server-published manifest before extraction, install, or launch.
- **CDN resilience layer** (ADR-0009) — a status-aware retry policy with exponential backoff + jitter and `Retry-After` honouring, plus a session **circuit breaker** that deprioritizes a failing CDN temporarily without ever dropping it (preserving full fallback).

### 🛠️ Changed

- **Per-CDN retries** — each CDN is now retried for transient failures before falling through, and the whole chain is swept multiple times with backoff before giving up. Size-only integrity validation rejects empty or truncated payloads.

### 🐛 Fixed

- **CDNs wrongly buried** — fixed proxy/mirror CDNs being marked unreachable for 6 hours when they returned a non-2xx to a `HEAD` probe while serving `GET` fine.
- **Transient blips** — timeouts, 5xx, 429, and TLS resets are now retried instead of failing the download outright.
- **GitHub token** — an expired/invalid `GITHUB_TOKEN` no longer kills the GitHub CDN tier; the request is replayed once anonymously.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture & Stability

### 🛠️ Changed

- **MainForm lifecycle** — `MainFormPresenter` is now the single owner of Dota 2 process monitoring and the operation pipeline; the form only reflects state.

### 🐛 Fixed

- **Graceful shutdown** — closing the window mid-install/patch now actually cancels and awaits the in-flight operation before closing, and the presenter, watchers, and tray service are now disposed unconditionally on close (previously leaked).

---

---

_For the full per-build breakdown, see [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md)._

name: 2.1.27-beta (Build 2136)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.27-beta

> Covers builds **2133 → 2136**

---

## ✨ Added

- **Base-Priority Method**: Added an optional per-hero `method` field to `heroes.json` (`1` = Base first, `2` = Base last) that explicitly overrides the `item_slot hero_base` auto-detection logic during mod generation.
- **Persona & Category Support**: Added full support for `Persona` mods (`persona_` prefix), slot tag extraction from archives (`ExtractItemTag()`), and integrated a 5th "Persona" section into the skin selector UI with a distinct magenta/pink theme.
- **UI/UX Indicators**: Added tag badges (`[shoulder]`, `[weapon]`, etc.) to item tiles and a "Cosmetic Compatibility Alert" prompt to warn when Base Hero mods are selected without matching sets.
- **Diagnostics**: Added detailed generation logs (priority source, method, layer order, and block count warnings) visible in the Visual Studio Output window to trace merge behavior.

## 🛠️ Changed & Improved

- **Layered Last-Writer-Wins Pipeline**: Reworked mod merging into a layered foundation-to-top pipeline (Base → Sets/Custom/Persona → Items). Every layer's blocks are written to `items_game.txt`, and later/lower layers override earlier ones for overlapping item IDs without dropping non-overlapping fields.
- **Mutual Exclusions**: Implemented tag-based mutual exclusion for items (selecting an item deselects other items with the same slot tag) and Persona mutual exclusion (selecting a Persona automatically clears and disables item/base selections).
- **Structure-Preserving Patcher**: Replaced KeyValues block patching with a structure-preserving overlay (`KeyValuesBlockHelper.OverlayBlockPreservingStructure`) that applies the custom `index.txt` block verbatim and carries over only essential base keys, eliminating modifier leaks and reordering bugs.
- **UI & Layout Optimizations**: Resized and optimized the Settings form and Support Dialog layouts to prevent overflow and scrollbars. Upgraded UI headers with native minimalist GDI+ vector icons.
- **Performance MVP Refactoring**: Decomposed `Dota2PerformanceForm` into `AutoexecService` and `Dota2PerformancePresenter` following strict MVP patterns, and secured `autoexec.cfg` saving with `FileTransactionService` atomic operations.
- **Documentation**: Re-aligned all 7 Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) to match current codebase patterns.

## 🐛 Fixed

- **Layer Drop Issues**: Fixed a bug where selected Sets/Items were dropped when a higher-priority Base claimed the same item IDs, and resolved a regression copying zero asset files.
- **Merge Priority Weights**: Fixed inverted layer sort weights in `HeroGenerationService` causing incorrect merge order.
- **Patcher Key Leakage**: Fixed items_game.txt patching reordering keys and leaking generic or duplicate modifier blocks into the game folder.
- **Settings Form Close Bug**: Fixed Settings form close (✕) button not responding due to drag-and-drop mouse capture.
- **WebView2 Timeouts**: Increased navigation timeouts to 15s to support slower client machines.

name: v2.1.26-beta (Build 2132)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.26-beta

> Covers builds **2131 → 2132**

---

## ✨ Added

- Skin Selector is available
- Introduced the `Persona` category (`persona_` prefix) for full hero model replacement mods, and a new slot tag extraction system for items.

## 🐛 Bug Fixed

- Update all configuration loading endpoints to use. If the app fails to connect to Cloudflare R2 (the primary CDN) for any reason, it will now automatically and silently fall back to:

- [ ] - jsDelivr CDN (Fallback 1)
- [ ] - GitHub Raw (Fallback 2)
- [ ] - GitHub Proxy Mirrors (Fallback 3 & 4)

- Fix In `HttpClientProvider.cs` we introduced a GitHubTokenHandler that filters outbound requests.

- Previously, if a user had a system-wide `TOKEN` environment variable, the application would attach it to every outbound request.
When sent to Cloudflare R2, Cloudflare rejected the request with `401 Unauthorized` or `403 Forbidden` error pages.
Now, the token is stripped and only sent to requests directed at GitHub domains.

name: v2.1.25-beta (Build 2131)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.25-beta

> Covers builds **2130 → 2131**

---

## ✨ Added

- Introduced the `SkinCategory` enum and classification logic based on ZIP archive name prefixes to automatically distinguish between *Legacy Sets*, *Custom Sets* (`mix_`), *Items* (`item_`), and *Base Heroes* (`base_`).
- Updated the UI to group skin options into distinct sections (Legacy Sets, Custom Sets, Items, Base Heroes) with themed icons, toggle indicators, and category headings.
- Replaced the simplified index/ID state with `HeroSelectionState` to model independent and concurrent selections of a Set, multiple Items, and a Base Hero.
- Enabled mapping and updating of Base Hero models using the `hero_base` item slot configuration.
- Added mode `--mode 4` to the model patching script showing a colored console summary of set classifications and stats per hero.

## 🛠️ Changed & Improved

- Re-engineered the mod generation pipeline to compile selected custom cosmetics in sequential hierarchy: Set → Items → Base Hero.
- **Selection Exclusivity Logic**:
  - Legacy Sets and Custom Sets share a single slot (mutual exclusion).
  - Base Hero is strictly single-select.
  - Items can be freely multi-selected and stacked.
- Changed storage behavior to save only highlighted hero IDs in preferences rather than specific item/set arrays, optimizing file footprint. Legacy selection formats are parsed automatically on restore.
- Unified raw hero configuration parsing into a single helper (`HeroModelMapper.MapFromSummaries`) to map configuration lists to `HeroModel` objects, eliminating 270+ lines of duplicate/legacy code in selectors and presenters.
- **Shared Utilities Extracted**:
  - Extracted helper methods `IsCustomSet()` and `FormatHeroIdAsName()` to the shared `HeroModelMapper`.
  - Consolidated cache cleanup logic into `HeroCacheHelper.Cleanup()`, removing duplicated procedures.
- Reverted `SixLabors.ImageSharp` to stable `3.1.12` and aligned testing dependencies to resolve license conflicts and compiler ambiguity.

## 🐛 Fixed

- Fixed a UI layout bug in `SelectHero.LoadPresetFromFile` where the selector queried active rows from `ScrollContainer` instead of `RowsFlow`, correcting preset activation.
- Deleted orphaned event handlers and dead design bindings in `SelectHero.Designer.cs`.

## 🧪 Testing

- Added a comprehensive unit test suite in `HeroModelMapperTests.cs` verifying `MapFromSummaries`, `IsCustomSet`, and formatting utilities across 17 distinct scenarios.

name: v2.1.22-beta (Build 2125)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.22-beta

> Covers builds **2122 → 2125**

---

## ✨ Added

- Added **Newcomer Onboarding Guide** — an interactive step-by-step overlay that highlights each feature when the app opens for the first time, guiding users through Auto Detect, Manual Detect, Skin Selector, Miscellaneous, Install, Patch Update, Console, and Settings.
- Onboarding uses a native WinForms spotlight overlay with pulsing cyan glow animations and dark backdrop for pixel-perfect control highlighting.
   - Features smooth spotlight transition animations, connector lines, dynamic tooltip heights, and L-bracket corner decorations.
   - Automatically captures a screenshot of the parent form to render actual UI behind a dimming layer.
- Onboarding state persists via `IConfigService` — shows only on first launch, can be re-triggered from Settings → "Show Guide" button.
- Added WebView2-based **Support Dialog** displaying Ko-fi donation goal and YouTube subscriber goal with CSS animations, powered by a remote `support_goals.json` config on R2 CDN.
- Created `SupportGoalsConfig` model and `SupportGoalsService` for fetching and caching combined goal data.

## 🚀 Improved

- Removed TailwindCSS and Google Fonts CDN dependencies — all CSS and fonts now load locally via `@font-face` for offline and GFW compatibility.
- Switched WebView2 from `NavigateToString` to `Navigate(file://)` to enable proper local font resolution.
- Added real-time input validation on numeric cvar fields with red border feedback and range tooltips (e.g., `fps_max` 0–999, `rate` 1000–1000000).
- Form now sizes responsively to 90% of screen bounds (min 900×600), fixing overflow on 1366×768 displays.
- Fixed inconsistent JSON escaping in settings load/apply by using `EscapeJs()` uniformly.
- Added proper `Dispose` override for WebView2 resource cleanup.
- Added GitHub proxy mirror fallbacks (`ghfast.top`, `gh-proxy.com`) as CDN priority 4–5 for users in regions where GitHub, Cloudflare R2, and jsDelivr are blocked by ISP-level filtering (e.g., China Mobile/Telecom behind the Great Firewall).
- Added `IsProxyUrl()` helper to `CdnConfig` for proxy domain detection, used by `CdnFallbackService` statistics tracking.
- `SmartCdnSelector` now recognizes and labels proxy CDNs in latency benchmark results.
- `CdnFallbackService` tracks proxy download successes separately in statistics for better observability.

## 📖 Documentation

- Comprehensive documentation refresh across 7 files to match current project state (v2.1.22-beta, 480+ tests).
- Updated version badge, test count (285+ → 480+), added Performance Tweaker feature, Courier/Ward/Special/Cursor mod types, Smart CDN Selection, Resumable Downloads, GFW proxy mirrors, and ADR-0004 Presenter Decomposition to design decisions.
- Added FAQ, Installer Guide, Security links; expanded ADR listing from 3 → 7; added all 9 API docs and `samples/` directory to structure tree.
- Rewrote all Mermaid diagrams — system architecture (5 presenters, 10 WebView2 forms, CDN/Misc service subgraphs), entry point flow (IMainFormFactory + DI), MVP class diagram (decomposed presenters), service layer (Courier/Ward/CDN services), and CDN table (GFW proxy entries + SmartCdnSelector).
- Replaced stale `ServiceLocator` test pattern with constructor injection + Moq; added STA apartment tip.
- Version 2.1.2 → 2.1.21, self-contained build (no .NET install), corrected install path to `%LocalAppData%`, added 6 missing misc categories, fixed settings path to `%AppData%`.
- Fixed project root name, added full 15-directory service tree, expanded CDN entry, added `exceptions.md` link.
- Removed .NET 8 install step (self-contained), added WebView2 troubleshooting tip.

## 🧪 Testing

- Added 14 unit tests for `Dota2PerformanceForm.ParseAutoexec` and `GenerateAutoexecContent` covering empty input, comments, standard cvars, inline comments, tabs, duplicates, alias lines, category grouping, unknown cvars, timestamping, and round-trip preservation.
- Added `InternalsVisibleTo` attribute to expose internal static methods to the test project.

---

name: v2.1.18-beta (Build 2118)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.18-beta

> Covers builds **2117 → 2118**

---

## 🐛 Fixed

- Fixed "Generation Failed: 404 (Not Found)" crash caused by unhandled `HttpRequestException` propagating on the final retry attempt, completely bypassing the CDN fallback system.
- `GetStringWithRetryAsync` and `GetByteArrayWithProgressAsync` now return `null` immediately on 404 instead of retrying 3 times and then crashing.
- `TryWithFallbackAsync` now catches `HttpRequestException` at each CDN level, ensuring R2 → jsDelivr → GitHub fallback actually works when a CDN returns an error.
- Misc generation now reports partial-success warnings (e.g., "Weather: Download failed") instead of silently skipping mods and showing "All mods successfully applied."
- Both WebView2 and classic Misc forms now display warning details and adjust the success dialog when mods were skipped.

---

## 🚀 Improved

- Misc and Hero skin downloads now use `SmartCdnSelector` benchmark results to auto-select the fastest CDN for each user, instead of hardcoded R2 → jsDelivr → GitHub order.
- Added Brotli decompression to `HttpClientProvider` — Cloudflare serves Brotli by default, 20-30% more efficient than gzip for JSON/text transfers.
- Replaced standalone `HttpClient` in `SubsGoalService` with shared `HttpClientProvider.Client` for consistent decompression, proxy, and TLS settings.

---

## 🛠️ Changed

- Updated `sync-to-r2.ps1` with `rclone copy` (safe mode), retry logic with backoff, per-folder Cache-Control headers (`max-age=86400` for Assets, `max-age=300` for config/remote), and post-sync cache verification + pre-warming.
- Replaced stale `pub-*.r2.dev` URL with custom domain `cdn.ardysamods.my.id` to avoid ISP blocking.
- Added `Check-CdnAssets.ps1` — validates all assets from `misc_config.json` + core files across R2, jsDelivr, and GitHub with performance ranking and failure reporting.

name: v2.1.14-beta (Build 2102)
body:
# Release Notes — v2.1.14

> Covers builds **2099 → 2102**
> Commits: `70b137c9` → `9bf317b0`

---

## ✨ New Features & Improvements

- Implemented build-aware update system that detects updates even when the version string remains the same (e.g., hotfixes re-uploaded to CDN).
- Added multi-CDN fallback (R2 → jsDelivr → GitHub Raw) with 30s stall-based failover to `OriginalVpkService` to reliably handle CDN unreachable/blocking states without endless stalls during base file downloads.
- Added multi-pattern build extraction from GitHub release notes and titles, supporting specific formats and ranges.
- Added robust version parsing logic via new `AppVersion` model to handle comparing semantic versions, pre-release suffixes, and build numbers.

---

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

- Removed `requireAdministrator` requirement from core application to prevent files written by the tool from inheriting admin ownership, which was causing Dota 2 to run in administrator mode and block matchmaking.
- Added `AdminHelper` utility to handle on-demand elevation only for legacy Program Files installations, maintaining write access backward compatibility.
- Fixed VPK item patching issue where rich `index.txt` data (e.g., Shadow Fiend arcana) wasn't being applied to `items_game.txt` due to unhandled one-liner formats.
- Improved `index.txt` discovery to fallback to the zip root when assets are stored in subfolders.
- Added notes-based fallback to R2 manifest parsing to gracefully extract build numbers from the `"notes"` field when an explicit `"build"` field is missing.
- Added comprehensive diagnostic logging for block parsing, validation, and replacement steps to aid debugging.
- Added complete test coverage for deeply nested block parsing, small-to-large block replacements, and double-tab formatting scenarios.
